name: sim_szilard_axis6_measurement_orientation_bridge
claim: Szilard measurement = Axis 6 'action orientation' selection. The demon's conditional operation is exactly the Axis 6 question: does operator A act on state rho from left (Arho) or right (rhoA)? Landauer erasure is orientation-independent. Measurement/erasure cycle is balanced.

pytorch.reason: W = k_B * T * ln(2); autograd dW/dT; verifies temperature-proportional work extraction; load-bearing

pyg.reason: not used — thermodynamic quantities are scalar/symbolic; no manifold sampling or persistent homology required for this Carnot/Szilard classical baseline

z3.reason: UNSAT: W > k_B*T*ln(2) for a single bit; no-free-lunch thermodynamic bound as z3 constraint; load-bearing

cvc5.reason: not used — thermodynamic quantities are scalar/symbolic; no manifold sampling or persistent homology required for this Carnot/Szilard classical baseline

sympy.reason: symbolic I(measurement) = k_B*ln(2); symbolic dS(erasure) = k_B*ln(2); verify I = dS (information-thermodynamic balance); load-bearing

clifford.reason: left-multiply vs right-multiply in Cl(2,0): e1*state vs state*e1; Axis 6 = this non-commutativity; load-bearing

geomstats.reason: not used — thermodynamic quantities are scalar/symbolic; no manifold sampling or persistent homology required for this Carnot/Szilard classical baseline

e3nn.reason: not used — thermodynamic quantities are scalar/symbolic; no manifold sampling or persistent homology required for this Carnot/Szilard classical baseline

rustworkx.reason: directed graph: measure node out-deg=2 (binary choice), erase node in-deg=2 (branches merge); load-bearing for Axis 6 graph structure

xgi.reason: hyperedge {measure, act_left, act_right, axis6} captures four-way measurement/orientation connection; load-bearing

toponetx.reason: not used — thermodynamic quantities are scalar/symbolic; no manifold sampling or persistent homology required for this Carnot/Szilard classical baseline

gudhi.reason: not used — thermodynamic quantities are scalar/symbolic; no manifold sampling or persistent homology required for this Carnot/Szilard classical baseline

P1_szilard_two_distinct_outcomes.note: Conditional operation has exactly 2 branches; each = distinct Axis 6 orientation

P2_landauer_erasure_orientation_independ.note: Erasure cost is identical regardless of which Axis 6 branch was taken

P3_measurement_erasure_balance.note: W = k_B*T*ln(2) = erasure cost => zero net entropy; information-thermodynamic balance

P4_sympy_information_thermodynamic_balan.note: Symbolic: I(measurement) = k_B*ln(2) = dS(erasure); balance is exact

P5_pytorch_work_temperature_proportional.note: autograd: dW/dT = k_B * ln(2) — work is linear in temperature (float32 tolerance 1e-6)

P6_clifford_axis6_noncommutativity.note: Left-multiply (Aρ) != right-multiply (ρA) in Cl(2,0) => Axis 6 orientation matters

P7_rustworkx_szilard_graph_structure.note: measure node splits to 2 branches (Axis 6 choice); erase merges both branches

P8_xgi_measurement_axis6_hyperedge.note: Four-way hyperedge {measure, act_left, act_right, axis6} captures the Axis 6 connection

N1_z3_no_free_lunch_unsat.note: UNSAT: W > k_B*T*ln(2) AND W <= k_B*T*ln(2) simultaneously => thermodynamic bound is hard

N2_no_measurement_no_net_work.note: Without measurement (random Axis 6 choice), net work is negative: erasure cost dominates

N3_sympy_landauer_entropy_T_independent.note: At T->0, W->0 but entropy per bit stays k_B*ln(2); energy cost vanishes but info cost does not

N4_clifford_scalar_state_commutes.note: Scalar state commutes with any op => Axis 6 is trivial (no orientation distinction) for scalar states

N5_zero_temperature_no_work.note: At T=0: W=0 and erasure cost=0; no thermodynamic activity; Axis 6 choice has zero consequence

B1_Landauer_energy_cost_to_zero.note: Landauer energy cost T*k_B*ln(2) -> 0 as T -> 0; entropy per bit stays k_B*ln(2)

B2_pytorch_constant_gradient.note: dW/dT = k_B*ln(2) is constant across all temperatures (linear in T)

B3_clifford_bivector_max_noncommutativit.note: Bivector state maximizes Axis 6 distinction: left-multiply != right-multiply maximally

B4_rustworkx_cyclic_szilard_is_not_dag.note: Repeated Szilard cycles form a cyclic graph (not a DAG); measure->act->erase->measure loop

B5_xgi_axis6_node_degree.note: Axis 6 node has degree 1 (appears in exactly one hyperedge — the measurement hyperedge)
